title: Race-lap pit-wave context from same-snapshot field state
summary: F1 pit calls are strongly relational: undercut, overcut, and cover-off behavior depend on how rival tyre age and pace compare on the same lap, not just on one driver’s raw features.
rationale: F1 pit calls are strongly relational: undercut, overcut, and cover-off behavior depend on how rival tyre age and pace compare on the same lap, not just on one driver’s raw features. Current best solutions are mostly row-local.
implementation_hint: Within each `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` snapshot, compute X-only context features such as tyre-life percentile, lap-delta z-score, degradation rank, compound-mix entropy, fraction of field already on later stints, and counts of nearby positions with older tyres or large positive `LapTime_Delta`. Feed these into the existing tabular model.
expected_effect: Better detection of collective pit windows and reactive stops when the surrounding field enters the undercut zone.
risk: Easy to create leakage if any aggregation accidentally crosses future laps; keep all context strictly same-lap and target-free.
